Engage.description: Hook the student, pose a real-world problem, activate prior knowledge.
Engage.instructions: Create an opening scenario, question, or media reference that connects the topic to the student's world. Activate curiosity without providing formal definitions.
Engage.tone: Conversational, curious, inviting — like a documentary opening

Explore.description: Guided inquiry, initial data analysis without formal definitions.
Explore.instructions: Present data, diagrams, or thought experiments that let the student investigate the concept. Avoid giving away the answer; let them discover patterns.
required_elements: Data table, diagram description, or virtual experiment setup
Explore.tone: Investigative, encouraging — like guiding a lab partner

Explain.instructions: Provide clear, structured explanations of key concepts and terminology. Use diagrams and examples to clarify. This is the core instructional phase.
required_elements: Formal definitions of all key terms
At least one worked example or analogy
Explain.tone: Clear, authoritative, pedagogically structured — like a textbook chapter

Elaborate.description: Application of knowledge to new domains, deeper practice.
Elaborate.instructions: Challenge students to apply their new knowledge in unfamiliar contexts. Include cross-topic connections and higher-order thinking questions.
required_elements: Extension activity or deeper investigation prompt
Elaborate.tone: Challenging, thought-provoking — like a university tutorial discussion

Evaluate.instructions: Test student understanding with structured assessment items aligned to the learning objectives. Include a mix of recall, application, and analysis questions.
required_elements: Summative-style questions at multiple cognitive levels
Mark scheme with explicit marking criteria
Evaluate.tone: Formal, precise — like an official exam paper
